title: MerLin: Photonic Quantum Machine Learning, Engineered for Utility - Quandela
why:
- MerLin 0.3 is an open-source framework that enables integration of photonic quantum circuits into modern machine learning workflows and facilitates scalable, differentiable training of quantum layers.
- The platform is specifically engineered for reproducible benchmarking, hardware-aware design, and the systematic evaluation of where quantum structure adds measurable utility in quantum machine learning.
- MerLin bridges photonic and qubit paradigms through a QuantumBridge abstraction, allowing qubit-based circuits to be mapped into photonic encodings and enabling controlled, cross-paradigm benchmarking.
- The framework comes with 18 fully reproduced state-of-the-art photonic and hybrid quantum machine learning models, supporting direct comparison under unified conditions.
- Built on Quandela’s Perceval photonic quantum SDK and optimized linear optics simulation, MerLin aims to transform photonic quantum machine learning from isolated demonstrations into an engineering discipline ready for utility and scale.
